title: acaena agnipila var. tenuispica
intro: Acaena agnipila var. tenuispica is a type of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to New Zealand. This plant, also known as the 'Bluebells of the Bush', has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Acaena agnipila var. tenuispica include its ability to reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation, making it beneficial for treating wounds, sprains, and strains. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used to create teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to promote healing and reduce the risk of infection.
